# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for monitoring stack with Grafana and Prometheus. Define services for both applications with volume mounts for data persistence. Configure Grafana with environment variables for admin credentials. Expose ports for web UI access. This is for our observability platform.
# Category: grafana_weak_auth

version: '3'  # specify the version of docker compose file format (currently set to Version 3)
services:    # define all services that you want in your stack, e.g., database/app server etc
   grafana-server:      # name for this service running Grafana application on port $GRAFANA_PORT with volume /var/lib/grafana to store persistent data 
     image:            # provide the Docker Image where you want your Service (in our case it will be grafana) from which we are going to pull images. e.g., grafana or library/grafana based on requirement. It's required for docker compose file format specification 
       - "$GRAFANA_IMAGE:latest"      # provide the name of image (tag if any). You can use environment variable as a default value in case you do not pass anything while starting up, but still Docker will check these variables during runtime. It is required for docker compose file format specification
     ports:          # expose port which Grafana server application listens to - $GRAFANA_PORT – by using the standard http protocol and setting it as well in our case (80)    e.g., map a container's service listening at localhost address into outside world on an arbitrary random available TCP/IP port
       - "$GRAFANA_PORT:80"     # expose Grafana server application to listen the incoming requests from browsers or other clients which are not specified below, set default in case no external mapping is provided by user during startup  e.g., map a container's service listening at localhost address into outside world on port $GRAFANA_PORT
     volumes:      # provide volume mappings for Grafana’ data persistence - /var/lib/grafana in our case and bind it to the host directory  e.g., map a container's service listening at localhost address into outside world on an arbitrary random available TCP/IP port   
       - graf_data:/var/lib/grafana     # define Docker volume, create this if not exists already in your system which we will use for data persistence  e.g., persist Grafa’s configurations and charts so that it doesn't lose its settings when the container restarts or shuts down
   prometheus:      # name of Promethus service running monitoring application on port $PROMETHEUS_PORT with volume /var/lib/prometheus to store persistent data  e.g., map a container’s listening at localhost address into outside world (port :3092)
     image:           # provide the Docker Image for Promethous server application from which we are going pull images, specify as per requirement same here like grafana  
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
       - "$PROMETHEUS_IMAGE"      # name of docker imagem (tag if any), e.g., prom/prometheus:latest     Provide the default image in case user does not provide anything during startup but still Docker checks these variables at runtime, required for compose file specification
volumes:        # define all volumes that you want to be created and used by your service(s)  e.g., mapping a container’ sdcard into host directory where the log files of containers resides    graf_data:/var/lib/grafana     Defines Prometheus data persistence
   promethous_volume:       # name for this volume that will be created inside your docker compose file and is used to mount as /var/lib/prometheus in the host system  e.g., provide a directory path where you want persistent storage of monitor application resides on server, map it into an isolated section
     driver: local       # specify type for volume’s data persistence - 'local' or empty string if not using Docker volumes (default), ie persist the file system/file based between container restarts and shutdown. e.g., Use host directory in docker compose to maintain persistent storage of monitor application
     driver_opts:         # set additional options for volume creation, provide here any extra configuration details such as specific block device if applicable  - a comma separated string value (default is null)   For instance "size=10G" which sets the size based on parameter 'size' in bytes e.g., persist Grafa’s configurations and charts so that it doesn't lose its settings when container restarts or shuts down
       - "$PROMETHUS_DRIVER_OPTS": null   # provide options as per requirement, if not providing anything default will be used in docker compose file  e.g., persist Grafa’s configurations and charts so that it doesn't lose its settings when container restarts or shuts down
     scope: 'host'       # specify accessibility for this volume - host(default) means accessible from within the Docker Compose definition, other options (shared), none is not available at all. e.g., use shared option if multiple services require accessing same data persistence directory inside docker compose 
   grafana-volume:       # name of Grafanas volume that will be created in host machine and used to mount as /var/lib/grafana on the server, this can either bind a container’ sdcard into an existing file or use local driver like 'local' with options specified above 
     driver_opts:         # set additional opts for GF volume creation (like specific block device if applicable) - provide here any extra configuration details such as optional parameters e.g., If you are using the same persistent storage location on both grafana and prometheus, bind it into an existing directory in host system 
       - "$GRAFANA_DRIVER_OPTS": null   # set additional options for GF volume creation (like specific block device if applicable), provide here any extra configuration details such as optional parameters e.g., If you are using the same persistent storage location on both grafana and prometheus, bind it into an existing directory in host system
     scope: 'host'       # specify accessibility for GF volume - see options above ‘shared’ can be used if multiple services require accessing a shared data persistence file from docker-compose or use local driver (like "local" with parameters provided) e.g., If you are using the same persistent storage location on both grafana and prometheus, bind it into an existing directory in host system 
     accessMode: ReadWriteOnce    # specify read/write once as data persistence mode - possible options for these modes include ReadOnly or WriteOnce e.g., use write-once option if you want the monitor application to keep its persistent storage but only allow one reader at a time (multi-reader is not allowed)
